title: NTC Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The dryer's NTC temperature sensor is providing out-of-range readings.
fullDescription: Error code E01 on Beko tumble dryers indicates the NTC thermistor monitoring the air or drum temperature is malfunctioning. The control board relies on this sensor to regulate heating and prevent overheating. If the readings are outside the expected range, the dryer stops as a safety precaution.

causes.cause: NTC sensor has failed (open or short circuit)
NTC sensor wiring is damaged
Sensor connector is corroded or loose
Control board sensor input circuit is faulty

symptoms: E01 error displayed on control panel
Dryer stops mid-cycle
Clothes come out damp or cold
Dryer overheats and shuts off
Error appears intermittently

fixSteps.title: Reset the dryer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer for 5 minutes. Reconnect and run a test cycle. If E01 returns, proceed with diagnosis.

fixSteps.title: Locate and test the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Access the NTC sensor (typically near the heating element or exhaust duct). Disconnect and measure resistance with a multimeter. At room temperature (25C), expect 10k-50k ohms depending on model.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring and connectors
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the wiring from the NTC sensor to the control board. Look for heat damage, corrosion, or loose connections.

fixSteps.title: Replace the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor is faulty, replace it with the correct part. Remove the mounting clip or screw, disconnect the old sensor, and connect the replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor and wiring test fine, the control board input circuit may need professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Beko dryer E01 NTC temperature sensor error fix

faq.q: Can a faulty NTC sensor cause the dryer to overheat?
faq.a: Yes. If the sensor reads lower than actual temperature, the heater stays on too long. The thermal fuse should provide secondary protection, but repair promptly.

faq.q: How many NTC sensors does a Beko dryer have?
faq.a: Most models have two: one monitoring inlet air temperature and one monitoring exhaust temperature. E01 typically relates to the primary sensor.

faq.q: Is the NTC sensor expensive?
faq.a: No, it is one of the cheapest parts at around 8-22 pounds/euros.

title: Heating Element Failure
shortDescription: The dryer heating element has failed or its circuit is open.
fullDescription: Error code E02 indicates the dryer's heating element is not producing heat. The control board has detected no temperature rise after activating the heater circuit. This could be a burned-out element, blown thermal fuse, or control board relay failure.

causes.cause: Heating element has burned out
Thermal fuse has blown (safety cutout)
High-limit thermostat has tripped
Control board heater relay has failed

symptoms: E02 error on display
Dryer runs but produces no heat
Clothes remain damp after full cycle
Dryer drum turns normally but air is cold
Cycle runs for maximum time without stopping

fixSteps.title: Check the thermal fuse
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the heating element housing (rear or side panel). Locate the thermal fuse and test for continuity. If open (no continuity), the fuse has blown and needs replacement.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the element wiring and test resistance across the terminals. Expect 20-50 ohms for a working element. Open circuit means the element is burned out.

fixSteps.title: Check the high-limit thermostat
fixSteps.instruction: Test the high-limit thermostat for continuity. If it has tripped, it may be resettable (press the red reset button) or it may need replacement.

fixSteps.title: Replace the failed component
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the thermal fuse, heating element, or thermostat as needed. If the thermal fuse has blown, investigate why (usually blocked lint or vent).

whenToCallPro: If all heating components test fine, the control board relay needs professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Beko dryer E02 heating element thermal fuse fix

faq.q: Why does the thermal fuse blow?
faq.a: Usually because of restricted airflow from a blocked lint filter, clogged condenser, or kinked exhaust vent. Always clean the filter before every cycle.

faq.q: Can I bypass the thermal fuse temporarily?
faq.a: This is extremely dangerous and must never be done. The thermal fuse prevents fire. Always replace it with the correct rating.

faq.q: How long should a Beko dryer heating element last?
faq.a: Typically 8-12 years with proper maintenance. Regular lint cleaning and vent maintenance extend element life.

title: Overheating Protection Triggered
shortDescription: The dryer has exceeded its maximum safe operating temperature.
fullDescription: Error code E03 indicates the dryer's overheat protection has been activated. The exhaust air temperature exceeded the safety threshold, causing the control board to shut down the heating element. This is usually caused by restricted airflow from blocked filters, vents, or condenser units.

causes.cause: Lint filter is severely clogged
Condenser unit is blocked with lint (condenser dryers)
Exhaust vent hose is kinked or blocked (vented dryers)
High-limit thermostat or thermal fuse has activated

symptoms: E03 error on display
Dryer is very hot to the touch
Burning smell from the dryer
Dryer shuts off mid-cycle
Clothes extremely hot when removed

fixSteps.title: Clean the lint filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and clean the lint filter thoroughly. Wash with warm soapy water if coated with fabric softener residue, which blocks airflow even when the filter looks clean.

fixSteps.title: Clean the condenser unit
fixSteps.instruction: For condenser dryers, remove the condenser (usually at the bottom front). Rinse under running water until all lint is removed. Allow to dry before reinstalling.

fixSteps.title: Check the exhaust vent
fixSteps.instruction: For vented dryers, check the exhaust hose for kinks and the external vent flap for blockages. Disconnect and vacuum out any accumulated lint.

fixSteps.title: Reset the thermostat
fixSteps.instruction: If a resettable high-limit thermostat has tripped, press the red reset button on the back of the dryer. If it trips again quickly, there is still an airflow restriction.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning all airflow paths does not resolve E03, there may be an internal duct blockage requiring professional cleaning.
videoSearchQuery: Beko dryer E03 overheating lint filter condenser fix

faq.q: Is E03 a fire risk?
faq.a: Potentially. Lint buildup combined with high heat is a leading cause of dryer fires. Clean the lint filter before every cycle and the condenser/vent monthly.

faq.q: How often should I clean the condenser?
faq.a: Beko recommends every 20 drying cycles, or at minimum once a month. More frequently if you dry lint-heavy items like towels.

faq.q: Can fabric softener sheets cause overheating?
faq.a: Fabric softener residue can coat the lint filter, blocking airflow even when the filter looks clean. Wash the filter with warm soapy water monthly.

title: Drive Motor Fault
shortDescription: The dryer's drum motor has failed or is not receiving power.
fullDescription: Error code E04 indicates the drum motor is not running when commanded. The control board has sent a signal to start the motor but no rotation is detected. This could be a motor failure, broken belt, faulty start capacitor, or door switch issue.

causes.cause: Drive belt has broken
Motor start capacitor has failed
Motor windings have an open circuit
Door switch not confirming closure

symptoms: E04 error on display
Drum does not turn
Motor hums but drum is stationary
No sound at all when cycle starts
Dryer starts briefly then stops

fixSteps.title: Check the door switch
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the door is closing fully and the latch clicks. Listen for the door switch clicking. If the switch is faulty, the motor will not start.

fixSteps.title: Check the drive belt
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the top or rear panel to access the drum. Check if the belt is intact and properly routed around the drum and motor pulley. A broken belt will be visible.

fixSteps.title: Test the motor capacitor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the start capacitor (cylindrical component near the motor). Discharge it safely, then test with a capacitance meter. Replace if the reading is significantly below the rated value.

fixSteps.title: Test the motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the motor wiring and test winding resistance with a multimeter. Expect 2-10 ohms between run winding terminals. Replace the motor if windings are open.

whenToCallPro: If the motor needs replacing, the job involves significant disassembly.
videoSearchQuery: Beko dryer E04 motor belt failure fix

faq.q: How do I know if the belt is broken?
faq.a: If the drum spins freely by hand with no resistance, the belt is broken. Normally you should feel the belt tension when turning the drum.

faq.q: Can I replace the belt myself?
faq.a: Yes, it is a common DIY repair. The belt wraps around the drum and threads through a tensioner pulley onto the motor shaft.

faq.q: Why does the motor hum but not spin?
faq.a: This usually indicates a failed start capacitor. The motor needs the capacitor for initial starting torque.

title: Door Switch / Lock Error
shortDescription: The door switch does not confirm that the dryer door is closed.
fullDescription: Error code E05 indicates the door switch circuit is not confirming door closure. The dryer will not operate with the door open as a safety measure. The door latch, switch, or wiring may be at fault.

causes.cause: Door latch is worn or broken
Door switch has failed
Door hinge is bent preventing proper closure
Wiring to door switch is damaged

symptoms: E05 error displayed
Dryer will not start
Door does not click when closed
Dryer starts then immediately stops
Interior light stays on (if equipped)

fixSteps.title: Check door closure
fixSteps.instruction: Close the door firmly. Check that the latch clicks into place. Look for any obstruction or clothing caught in the door seal.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the latch
fixSteps.instruction: Examine the door latch prong and the strike plate. If the latch is worn or the strike is misaligned, the switch will not activate.

fixSteps.title: Test the door switch
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the switch behind the front panel. Test with a multimeter: the switch should show continuity when the door is closed (latch engaged).

fixSteps.title: Replace the door switch
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the switch from its mounting, disconnect the wires (note positions), and install the replacement.

whenToCallPro: Rarely needed for E05. Door switch replacement is straightforward.
videoSearchQuery: Beko dryer E05 door switch latch error fix

faq.q: Can I tape the door switch closed?
faq.a: Never bypass the door switch. It is a critical safety device that prevents the dryer from running with the door open, which could cause burns or entanglement injuries.

faq.q: Why does my door not latch anymore?
faq.a: The plastic latch prong wears over time from repeated opening and closing. It is a cheap and easy replacement part.

faq.q: Does the dryer light staying on confirm a door switch issue?
faq.a: If the interior light stays on with the door closed, the switch is likely not detecting closure.

title: Condenser Water Tank Full
shortDescription: The condensation water collection tank is full and needs emptying.
fullDescription: Error code E07 on Beko condenser dryers indicates the water collection tank has reached its maximum level. The float switch in the tank has triggered, stopping the dryer to prevent overflow. This is a normal maintenance alert rather than a fault.

causes.cause: Water tank is full and needs emptying
Tank float switch is stuck in the raised position
Condensation pump has failed (pump-equipped models)
Tank is not seated properly after emptying

symptoms: E07 or water tank indicator on display
Dryer stops mid-cycle
Water tank is visibly full
Cycle will not restart until tank is emptied
Error appears every few cycles

fixSteps.title: Empty the water tank
fixSteps.instruction: Pull out the water collection tank from the top of the dryer. Empty into a sink. Replace the tank, ensuring it clicks into position.

fixSteps.title: Check the float switch
fixSteps.instruction: If the tank was not full but E07 appeared, remove the tank and check the float mechanism. It should move freely up and down. Clean if debris is preventing movement.

fixSteps.title: Clean the tank and housing
fixSteps.instruction: Wash the tank with warm water. Clean the tank housing slot and the float switch contact area to ensure proper seating and detection.

fixSteps.title: Check condensation pump
fixSteps.instruction: If your model has an automatic pump to the tank, listen for the pump running. If not, the pump may be blocked or failed. Access from behind the front panel.

whenToCallPro: Only if the condensation pump is faulty. Otherwise, E07 is a simple maintenance task.
videoSearchQuery: Beko dryer E07 water tank full error fix

faq.q: Can I connect a drain hose to avoid emptying the tank?
faq.a: Yes. Most Beko condenser dryers have an optional drain hose connection. Route a hose from the drain port to a nearby sink or floor drain for automatic drainage.

faq.q: How often does the tank need emptying?
faq.a: Typically after every 1-3 cycles depending on load size and dampness. A full load of wet towels produces significantly more condensation.

faq.q: Why is the tank not filling but E07 still appears?
faq.a: The float switch may be stuck or the tank may not be seated correctly. Remove, clean, and reseat the tank firmly.

title: Compressor Fault (Heat Pump Dryers)
shortDescription: The heat pump compressor has failed or is not operating correctly.
fullDescription: Error code E08 on Beko heat pump dryers indicates a compressor fault. The compressor is the core of the heat pump system that recycles heat for energy-efficient drying. If the compressor fails to start, draws excessive current, or does not maintain proper pressure, this error is triggered.

causes.cause: Compressor start relay or capacitor has failed
Compressor motor has seized
Refrigerant leak reducing system pressure
Inverter board controlling the compressor has failed

symptoms: E08 error on display
Dryer runs but produces very little heat
Clicking sounds from the rear of the machine
Dryer draws high current then trips the breaker
Compressor area is very hot

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 15 minutes to allow the compressor protection relay to reset. Reconnect and try again.

fixSteps.title: Clean the heat exchanger
fixSteps.instruction: A dirty heat exchanger can cause the compressor to overwork and overheat. Clean the condenser and evaporator coils with a soft brush.

fixSteps.title: Check for airflow restrictions
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the lint filter is clean and all air ducts are clear. Restricted airflow overloads the heat pump system.

fixSteps.title: Professional compressor diagnosis
fixSteps.instruction: If E08 persists after cleaning, the compressor, start components, or inverter board need professional diagnosis. This involves testing compressor motor resistance and system pressures.

whenToCallPro: E08 almost always requires professional repair. The heat pump system contains refrigerant that requires certified handling.
videoSearchQuery: Beko heat pump dryer E08 compressor error fix

faq.q: Is heat pump compressor repair expensive?
faq.a: It can be. Compressor replacement costs 150-350 for the part plus labour. However, the dryer itself is a premium product, so repair is usually worthwhile if under 5-6 years old.

faq.q: Can I convert a heat pump dryer to a conventional heater?
faq.a: No. Heat pump dryers are designed around the heat pump system and cannot be converted to conventional heating.

faq.q: How long should a heat pump compressor last?
faq.a: A well-maintained heat pump compressor should last 10-15 years. Regular filter and condenser cleaning is essential.

title: Humidity Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The drum humidity sensor used for auto-dry programs is malfunctioning.
fullDescription: Error code E09 indicates the humidity sensor bars inside the drum are not functioning correctly. These metal bars detect the moisture level of the laundry to determine when the drying cycle should end. If the sensor readings are invalid, auto-dry programs cannot function properly.

causes.cause: Humidity sensor bars are coated with fabric softener residue
Humidity sensor wiring is disconnected
Humidity sensor has failed electrically
Control board sensor circuit is faulty

symptoms: E09 error on display
Auto-dry programs run too long or too short
Clothes either over-dried or still damp
Only timed programs work correctly
Error appears only on auto-sensor programs

fixSteps.title: Clean the humidity sensors
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the two metal sensor bars inside the drum (usually near the lint filter area). Clean them with a cloth dampened with white vinegar to remove fabric softener residue and limescale.

fixSteps.title: Run a timed cycle
fixSteps.instruction: After cleaning, run a timed dry cycle to verify the drum and heater work correctly. Then try an auto-sensor cycle to see if E09 has cleared.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Remove the front panel or drum access panel. Trace the sensor wires from the bars to the connector. Ensure they are secure.

fixSteps.title: Test sensor resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor wiring and test resistance between the bars. With dry bars, expect very high resistance (megohms). With damp cloth touching both bars, resistance should drop significantly.

whenToCallPro: Rarely needed. Cleaning the sensor bars resolves the issue in most cases.
videoSearchQuery: Beko dryer E09 humidity sensor cleaning fix

faq.q: How do I find the humidity sensors?
faq.a: Look inside the drum near the lint filter slot. You will see two small metal bars (usually stainless steel) about 5-10cm long, mounted parallel to each other.

faq.q: Does fabric softener affect the sensors?
faq.a: Yes. Fabric softener (liquid or dryer sheets) leaves a waxy residue on the sensor bars that insulates them, preventing accurate moisture detection. Clean monthly.

faq.q: Can I still use timed dry programs with E09?
faq.a: Yes. Timed programs do not rely on the humidity sensor. Select a timed program as a workaround until the sensor is cleaned or replaced.

title: Control Board / PCB Fault
shortDescription: The main control board has detected an internal fault.
fullDescription: Error code E10 indicates the main control board (PCB) has an internal hardware or firmware error. The microprocessor has detected an issue during its self-diagnostic routine and cannot continue normal operation.

causes.cause: Power surge has damaged the PCB
EEPROM data corruption
Dry solder joints on the PCB
Component failure on the board

symptoms: E10 error on display at startup
Dryer will not respond to any controls
Display shows random characters then E10
Error cannot be cleared by power reset
Dryer was working fine before a power outage

fixSteps.title: Extended power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 30 minutes for full capacitor discharge. Reconnect and check if E10 clears.

fixSteps.title: Check mains voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Verify mains voltage is within specification (220-240V AC). Unstable supply can cause board issues.

fixSteps.title: Visual board inspection
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the PCB cover and inspect for burnt components, bulging capacitors, or scorch marks.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: If E10 persists, the PCB needs replacement. Note the exact part number from the board label before ordering.

whenToCallPro: E10 almost always requires professional diagnosis and board replacement or repair.
videoSearchQuery: Beko dryer E10 control board PCB replacement

faq.q: Can the board be repaired instead of replaced?
faq.a: Often yes. Specialist PCB repair services can fix specific component failures for 40-80 pounds, cheaper than a new board.

faq.q: Will a surge protector prevent E10?
faq.a: A good surge protector significantly reduces the risk of PCB damage from power surges and is recommended for all modern appliances.

faq.q: How long does a control board last?
faq.a: Typically the lifetime of the appliance (10-15 years). Power surges and moisture are the main causes of premature failure.

title: Fan Motor Fault
shortDescription: The dryer's blower fan motor is not running or is running at incorrect speed.
fullDescription: Error code E11 indicates the blower fan motor that circulates air through the drum and heating system is not operating correctly. Without proper airflow, the dryer cannot dry clothes and risks overheating. The fan motor, wiring, or control circuit may be at fault.

causes.cause: Fan motor has failed
Fan blade is obstructed by lint or debris
Fan motor wiring is disconnected
Control board fan circuit has failed

symptoms: E11 error on display
No airflow felt at the vent outlet
Dryer overheats quickly
Clothes take extremely long to dry
Loud or unusual sounds from the fan area

fixSteps.title: Check for lint buildup
fixSteps.instruction: Clean the lint filter thoroughly. Also check inside the fan housing for accumulated lint that may be restricting the fan blade.

fixSteps.title: Listen for the fan
fixSteps.instruction: Start the dryer and listen for the fan running. If you hear the drum turning but no fan, the motor or blade may be faulty.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the fan blade
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power and access the fan assembly. Check that the blade is intact, not cracked, and spins freely on the shaft.

fixSteps.title: Test the fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the motor wiring and test winding resistance. Replace if open circuit.

whenToCallPro: If the fan motor needs replacing and you cannot access it through the available panels.
videoSearchQuery: Beko dryer E11 fan motor error fix

faq.q: Is a broken fan blade a fire risk?
faq.a: Yes. Without proper airflow, the heating element can overheat the surrounding components and lint, creating a fire risk. Stop using the dryer until repaired.

faq.q: Can lint cause the fan motor to fail?
faq.a: Yes. Lint wrapping around the fan shaft increases friction and motor load, eventually burning out the motor. Regular cleaning prevents this.

faq.q: Is the fan motor the same as the drum motor?
faq.a: On some models they are the same motor driving both drum and fan. On others, they are separate motors. Check your model specifications.

title: Communication Error Between Boards
shortDescription: The display board and main control board cannot communicate.
fullDescription: Error code E13 indicates a communication failure between the display/interface board and the main control board. These boards exchange data over a serial link, and if the connection is interrupted, the dryer cannot accept commands or display information correctly.

causes.cause: Ribbon cable or connector between boards is loose
Display board has failed
Main control board communication port is faulty
Connector pins are corroded

symptoms: E13 error on display (if partially working)
Blank or flickering display
Buttons do not respond
Random program selection
Dryer starts but cannot be controlled

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes and reconnect.

fixSteps.title: Reseat the cable
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the top panel to access the wiring between display and main boards. Disconnect and firmly reseat the ribbon cable or connector at both ends.

fixSteps.title: Inspect connectors
fixSteps.instruction: Check pins for corrosion or bending. Clean with contact cleaner if needed.

fixSteps.title: Replace the display board
fixSteps.instruction: If the cable is fine, try replacing the display board first (cheaper than the main board).

whenToCallPro: If reseating cables does not fix the issue, professional diagnosis of which board is faulty is needed.
videoSearchQuery: Beko dryer E13 display communication error fix

faq.q: Is E13 usually the cable or the board?
faq.a: Most commonly it is a loose cable or corroded connector. This should always be checked first before replacing any boards.

faq.q: Can moisture cause E13?
faq.a: Yes. If the dryer is in a humid environment, moisture on connectors can disrupt communication. Ensure good ventilation.

faq.q: Can I swap boards from another dryer?
faq.a: Only if it is the same model and firmware version. Mismatched boards will not communicate correctly.
